data.title: AI fears rattle software loans, but investors pick winners and losers: WSJ
data.description:
Artificial intelligence worries have shaken the market for software-company loans, fueling sharp declines and renewed concerns about credit risk. But investors aren’t treating every company the same, The Wall Street Journal reported.

There have wide differences in performance among more than 100 first-lien software loans since late January, a Journal review found. Some borrowers have held up relatively well, while others have been hit hard as investors reassess which businesses can withstand AI disruption.

Software loans make up about 13% of the Morningstar LSTA US Leveraged Loan Index and an even larger portion of many private-credit portfolios, largely because of past private-equity buyouts.

Among major categories, vertical software companies performed best, with loans down an average of 4.2 cents on the dollar. These firms serve specialized industries and often handle sensitive workflows, making customers less likely to switch providers. Examples include CCC Intelligent Solutions, Relativity and RealPage.

Cybersecurity loans were next, down 5.3 cents on average. Many investors believe rising cyber threats could increase demand for security products, helping companies such as Proofpoint and Gen Digital.

Horizontal software loans, which cover broad business functions, fell 8.8 cents on average. Investors favored companies tied to payroll, tax compliance and infrastructure, while showing more caution toward data analytics and document automation firms.

The weakest group was software-engineering tools, down 16.3 cents on average. Businesses that help developers write or manage code are viewed as especially exposed because AI can increasingly perform similar tasks. Loans tied to Idera, SmartBear and Perforce dropped below 80 cents, signaling elevated default concerns.

Loan quality also mattered. Names trading above 95 cents on the dollar in January fell 4.5 cents on average, while weaker credits already below that level lost 10.5 cents.

Investors say many judgments remain uncertain, as the full impact of AI on software businesses is still unfolding.

data.title: Earnings week ahead: MSFT, AAPL, GOOG, AMZN, META, XOM, CVX, KO, V, SBUX, F, GM, and more
data.description:
As earnings season hits its peak, the week ahead stands out as one of the most consequential reporting stretches of the year.

Five members of the Magnificent Seven, Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), and Meta (META), headline the calendar, placing a sharp spotlight on AI monetization, cloud demand, digital advertising, and consumer ecosystems.

Beyond big tech, the week spans a remarkably broad cross-section of the global economy.

Energy majors Exxon Mobil (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and ConocoPhillips (COP) will reflect commodity dynamics, while industrial bellwethers Caterpillar (CAT), General Dynamics (GD), and UPS offer a read on infrastructure, defense, and global logistics demand.

Consumer and payments giants like Coca-Cola (KO), Starbucks (SBUX), Chipotle (CMG), Visa (V), and Mastercard (MA) will provide cues on spending resilience, and healthcare leaders Eli Lilly (LLY), Merck (MRK), AbbVie (ABBV), and Amgen (AMGN) will highlight innovation pipelines and demand durability.

The calendar further extends into autos, telecom, fintech, and emerging tech, with Ford (F), GM, T-Mobile (TMUS), Verizon (VZ), Robinhood (HOOD), Roku (ROKU), and Enphase Energy (ENPH) adding depth across connectivity, retail investing, and clean energy.

Rounding out the week are names spanning digital platforms, materials, and semiconductors, including QUALCOMM (QCOM), Spotify (SPOT), Booking Holdings (BKNG), First Solar (FSLR), and Vale (VALE).

With leadership stocks and cyclical sectors reporting side by side, this week's earnings will deliver one of the most comprehensive snapshots yet of global growth, corporate spending, and market direction, and a critical test of whether elevated expectations around AI, consumption, and macro resilience can hold.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of April 20 to April 24:

Monday, April 27

Verizon Communications (VZ)

Verizon Communications (VZ) is set to report Q1 results before Monday’s open, with analysts expecting modest Y/Y growth in both revenue and earnings.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains more cautious at Hold, citing limited growth visibility.

SA views remain mixed:

Kenio Fontes recently downgraded the stock to Hold, pointing to modest near-term momentum (2–3% revenue growth and 4–5% EPS guidance for 2026) amid intense competition from T-Mobile US and AT&T. 

On the bullish side, The Alpha Analyst reiterates a Strong Buy, viewing VZ as an income-focused play supported by resilient cash flows, ~9x forward earnings valuation, and robust capital return plans. Management’s focus on ~$5B in cost savings, disciplined capex, and steady free cash flow growth underpins dividend sustainability and buybacks.

In contrast, SA Investing Group Leader Stone Fox Capital maintains a Sell, citing structural growth challenges, high leverage, and limited exposure to next-generation growth areas. Despite its ~6% yield, concerns around flat wireless growth and reliance on legacy assets could pressure long-term valuation.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.21
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $34.86B
  • Earnings Insight: The company has exceeded EPS expectations in all the past 8 quarters and revenue estimates 50% of the time in that span.

Also reporting: Nucor (NUE), Ventas (VTR), Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY), NOV (NOV), Domino's Pizza (DPZ), Public Storage (PSA), AvalonBay Communities (AVB), Cadence Design Systems (CDNS), Lending Club (LC), Alliance Resource Partners (ARLP), Noble Corporation (NE), Easterly Government Properties (DEA), and more.

Tuesday, April 28

Coca-Cola (KO)

The Coca-Cola Company (KO) is set to report Q1 results before Tuesday’s open, with analysts expecting over 9% Y/Y growth in both revenue and earnings.

The company recently announced a $1B investment in South Africa through 2030, alongside local bottling partners, aimed at expanding production, strengthening distribution, and driving product innovation, highlighting its asset-light, franchise-driven model.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system is more cautious at Hold, citing valuation concerns.

SA author YR Research reiterates a Buy, emphasizing Coca-Cola’s defensive appeal, consistent mid-single-digit organic growth, and strong dividend profile, with expected annual returns of 8–10%.

In contrast, SA Investing Group Leader Dhierin Bechai downgraded the stock to Hold, noting limited upside after recent outperformance, with a price target implying modest gains despite stable cash flows and resilient fundamentals.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.81
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $12.24B
  • Earnings Insight: The beverage giant has beaten EPS expectations in 8 straight quarters and revenue in 7 of those reports.

Also reporting: Visa (V), Starbucks (SBUX), Enterprise Products Partners L.P. (EPD), General Motors (GM), BP p.l.c. (BP), Omega HealthCare Investors (OHI), Expand Energy Corporation (EXE), W. P. Carey Inc. (WPC), United Parcel Service (UPS), Ares Capital (ARCC), American Tower Corp. (AMT), Enphase Energy (ENPH), Corning (GLW), Novartis International AG (NVS), STAG Industrial (STAG), WM (WM), Spotify Technology S.A. (SPOT), ONEOK (OKE), NXP Semiconductors N.V. (NXPI), Kimberly-Clark (KMB), Booking Holdings (BKNG), T-Mobile US (TMUS), Seagate Technology (STX), Mondelez International (MDLZ), Welltower (WELL), JetBlue Airways (JBLU), Opko Health (OPK), Two Harbors Investment (TWO), Teradyne (TER), SYSCO (SYY), Bloom Energy (BE), S&P Global (SPGI), Robinhood Markets (HOOD), and more.

Wednesday, April 29

Microsoft Corporation (MSFT), Amazon.com (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META), and Alphabet (GOOG)

Wednesday shapes up as a pivotal moment for Big Tech, with four “Magnificent 7” names. Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), Amazon (AMZN), and Alphabet (GOOGL) are all set to report after the market closes, placing the spotlight firmly on AI, cloud, and digital advertising trends.

Heading into earnings, the narrative remains centered on AI-driven growth versus margin pressure. Microsoft’s Azure momentum is key, though rising CapEx continues to divide opinion. Meta is viewed constructively on pullbacks despite mixed reception to recent AI developments, while Alphabet’s steady growth is tempered by heavy investments in AI and adjacent ventures. Amazon, meanwhile, is doubling down on AI and infrastructure through its massive AWS investments, with markets closely watching returns on capital.

Wall Street maintains a Strong Buy across all four names, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system takes a more measured stance with Hold ratings, reflecting valuation concerns.

However, SA author Gary Alexander rates Amazon a Sell, arguing its ~$2.7T valuation is difficult to justify despite recent momentum driven by AWS and AI partnerships. 

Similarly, Dmytro Lebid sees Microsoft as a Sell, citing rising capital intensity, margin pressure, and potential multiple compression as the business shifts toward infrastructure-heavy AI investments.

Here’s a preview of the key information ahead of the reports:

Also reporting: Google (GOOGL), Ford Motor (F), AbbVie (ABBV), Qualcomm (QCOM), Teladoc Health (TDOC), SoFi (SOFI), Teva Pharmaceutical Industries (TEVA), Etsy (ETSY), GlaxoSmithKline (GSK), Phillips 66 (PSX), Biogen (BIIB), Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG), AstraZeneca (AZN), Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN), Sundial Growers (SNDL), Lemonade (LMND), General Dynamics (GD), eBay (EBAY), and more.

Thursday, April 30

Apple (AAPL)

California-based Apple (AAPL), another core member of the “Magnificent Seven,” is set to report FQ2 results after Thursday’s close, with analysts expecting ~18% Y/Y EPS growth on ~15% revenue expansion.

Recent developments have kept the spotlight on the iPhone maker. A major leadership transition is underway, with Tim Cook set to move into an Executive Chairman role and John Ternus stepping in as CEO, signaling a stronger product and engineering focus.

Meanwhile, iPhone shipments in China rose 20% in Q1, supported by the latest iPhone cycle, pricing actions, and government incentives.

Wall Street remains bullish, with Morgan Stanley viewing the upcoming print as a potential “clearing event,” driven by solid momentum across iPhone, Mac, and Services.

However, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating stays at Hold, citing valuation concerns.

SA views are notably divided.

Louis Gerard rates the stock a Sell, flagging elevated multiples, AI execution risks, and potential margin pressure, while Given Mahlangu maintains a Strong Buy, pointing to Apple’s pricing advantage and resilient demand dynamics.

Bill Maurer adopts a wait-and-watch stance, highlighting strong fundamentals but cautioning that premium valuation may limit near-term upside.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.95
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $109.69B
  • Earnings Insight: Apple has beaten EPS and revenue expectations in 8 straight quarters.

Also reporting: Altria Group (MO), Merck & Co. (MRK), Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY), Mastercard (MA), Caterpillar (CAT), Roku (ROKU), Twilio (TWLO), Amgen (AMGN), ConocoPhillips (COP), Iron Mountain (IRM), Southern Company (SO), Roblox (RBLX), Valero Energy (VLO), First Solar (FSLR), Eli Lilly & Co. (LLY), Riot Platforms (RIOT), Illumina (ILMN), SiriusXM Holdings (SIRI), Western Digital (WDC), Carrier Global (CARR), Agnico-Eagle Mines (AEM), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), American International Group (AIG), Tanger Factory Outlet Centers (SKT), Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL), Clorox (CLX), Cardinal Health (CAH), Atlassian (TEAM), T. Rowe Price Group (TROW), and more.

Friday, May 1

Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX)

Chevron Corporation (CVX) and Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM) are all set to report Q1 results before Friday’s open, with analysts expecting a mixed performance amid a volatile energy backdrop.

Recent developments highlight diverging narratives. Exxon has advanced its LNG ambitions, with its Golden Pass project shipping its first cargo, while also exploring downstream portfolio optimization, including a potential sale of its Hong Kong retail network. However, Wolfe Research recently downgraded the stock, citing valuation concerns after its strong run.

Chevron, meanwhile, has resumed full production at its Wheatstone LNG facility following outage-related disruptions, though operational challenges, including a refinery incident in Texas, could weigh on near-term downstream performance.

Sentiment remains broadly constructive. Wall Street maintains Buy ratings on both names, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system assigns Strong Buy ratings, supported by elevated oil prices and geopolitical tailwinds.

SA author views reflect this divergence. Asian Value Investor maintains a Strong Buy on Chevron, citing its upstream leverage, strong balance sheet, and resilience to higher oil prices. In contrast, Exxon is rated Hold, with solid fundamentals largely priced in and technical indicators signaling caution despite continued operational strength.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: XOM: $1.0; CVX: $1.0
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: XOM: $85.26B; CVX: $52.70B
  • Earnings Insight: XOM has topped EPS expectations in 7 of the past 8 quarters, beating revenue estimates in 6 of those reports, whereas CVX has beaten EPS in 6 and revenue estimates in 5 of those reports.
Also reporting: Moderna (MRNA), Dominion Energy (D), Colgate-Palmolive (CL), Brookfield Renewable Partners (BEP), Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT), TC Energy (TRP), LyondellBasell Industries (LYB), Magna International (MGA), Linde plc (LIN), Estée Lauder Companies (EL), Newell Brands (NWL), HF Sinclair Corporation (DINO), Church & Dwight (CHD), AON Plc (AON), Lazard (LAZ), Cinemark (CNK), AutoNation (AN), Cboe Global Markets (CBOE), and more.

data.title: Trump safe after shots fired at White House Correspondents' Dinner hotel
data.description:
• President Trump is safe after a gunman apparently tried to breach security at the Washington Hilton where the White House Correspondents' Dinner was taking place Saturday night.
  • The president was on the main dais in the hotel's main ballroom at the time. The shots were not fired in the ballroom itself.
  • Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were immediately evacuated from the ballroom, along with Vice President JD Vance and members of the Cabinet who were in attendance.
  • In a Truth Social post, Trump said that the shooter is in custody. He also praised the Secret Service and law enforcement.
  • Despite the shooting, Trump wants the dinner to continue.
  • "I have recommended that we "LET THE SHOW GO ON" but, will entirely be guided by Law Enforcement. They will make a decision shortly. Regardless of that decision, the evening will be much different than planned, and we'll just, plain, have to do it again."

This is breaking news and will be updated.

data.title: U.S. shale industry reluctant to boost oil production in response to Iran war 'chaos'
data.description:
U.S. shale executives say they do not expect to significantly increase production over the next two years as a result of the "chaos" arising from the war in the Middle East.

According to the anonymous survey of more than 100 oil and gas companies published this week in a quarterly report released by the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas, 43% of executives said they do not expect daily production to increase by more than 250K bbl/day in 2026, and 32% of respondents said they expected production to rise more than 250K bbl/day but not more than 500K bbl/day in 2027.

The industry's reluctance to commit to production increases comes despite President Trump's "energy dominance" agenda, and as Secretary of Energy Chris Wright and Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum recently held a call with oil and gas executives to encourage more production.

Yet despite the Trump administration's pressure, U.S. oil and gas rig counts have remained flat, apparently a sign of the sector's more disciplined approach after previous boom and bust cycles.

In comments published with the survey, one executive said: "With all of the chaos,predicting anything in the energy sector is very difficult."

"The difference between the gyration of paper market oil prices versus what seems to be substantially higher physical prices sends conflicting signals to operators who cannot plan rigs and capital budgets when prices swing wildly based on tweets," another respondent said.

"The long-term consequences of this war were not fully considered," an executive from an unnamed oilfield services firm said. "The disruption this will cause to energy markets and other macroeconomic measures will be significant. The unpredictable nature of the current administration makes business modeling near impossible."

The Strait of Hormuz remains mostly closed, trapping a fifth of the world's crude supplies, and ~80% of respondents to the survey expect traffic to normalize no earlier than August, while two-thirds said they expect 90% of trapped Persian Gulf production to return to market eventually.

ETFs: (XLE), (XOP), (VDE), (IXC), (OIH), (CRAK), (AMLP)

data.title: Trump calls off Witkoff, Kushner trip to Pakistan
data.description:
• President Trump Saturday afternoon said that Special Envoy to the Middle East Steve Witkoff and Special Envoy for Peace Missions Jared Kushner would not be travelling to Pakistan for talks to end the Iran War as planned.
  • "I’ve told my people a little while ago, they were getting ready to leave, and I said, 'Nope, you’re not making an 18-hour flight to go there. We have all the cards," Trump said in a statement. "They can call us anytime they want, but you’re not going to be making any more 18-hour flights to sit around talking about nothing."
  • "Besides which, there is tremendous infighting and confusion within their "leadership," the president added in a Truth Social post. "Nobody knows who is in charge, including them." 

More on the U.S.-Iran war

  • Oil And Equity Markets: Someone Is Lying
  • Oil Is A Critical War Gauge For Tracking Iran Risk In Real Time
  • The Bullish Case For Commodities Remains Intact
  • Hormuz standoff puts pressure on allies to step up, Pete Hegseth says - Bloomberg
  • Crude oil gains for the fourth straight day as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate further

data.title: Key deals this week: Helix Energy, Tesla, QXO, USA Rare Earth and more
data.description:
Here's a list of key deals reported across sectors this week:

  • Helix Energy Solutions (HLX) and Hornbeck Offshore Services (HOS) said Thursday they agreed to merge in an all-stock deal, creating a premier integrated offshore services company.
  • Tesla (TSLA) disclosed in a regulatory filing an agreement to acquire an unnamed AI hardware company for up to $2B in Tesla common stock and equity awards.
  • KeyCorp (KEY) is set to acquire the UK-based Clearwater Corporate Finance, marking the diversified bank's entry into the Western European market.
  • Eli Lilly (LLY) on Monday announced an agreement to acquire Kelonia Therapeutics, a privately held cancer drug developer, in a deal worth up to $7B in cash.
  • Sila Realty Trust (SILA) stock was surging after the net lease REIT announced a definitive merger agreement with certain affiliates of Blue Owl Capital (OWL) involving an all-cash transaction valued at ~$2.4B.
  • USA Rare Earth (USAR) down 3.8% pre-market Monday after saying it agreed to acquire Brazilian rare earths miner Serra Verde Group for $300M in cash and 126.85M common shares, implying an equity value of ~$2.8B.
  • QXO Inc. (QXO) on Sunday said it has agreed to acquire TopBuild Corp. (BLD) in a transaction valued at about $17 billion, a move that would broaden its presence across several building products categories in North America.
  • Teleflex (TFX) rose 9.7% after a report that CVC Capital Partners and GTCR teamed up for a takeover offer for the medical equipment provider. The PE firms have been working together to consider a possible takeover for Teleflex (TFX), according to a Bloomberg report late Tuesday.

data.title: Good entry point for Berkshire Hathaway after underperformance - Barron's
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) shares have fallen from expensive to attractively priced over the past year, creating a potential buying opportunity even as the conglomerate transitions to new leadership under CEO Greg Abel, according to a Barron's article.

The Class A shares are down 6% year-to-date to $706,000, while the Class B shares have declined similarly to $470. More strikingly, Berkshire has dropped about 13% since peaking on May 2, 2025 — the day before Warren Buffett announced his year-end retirement as CEO. During that same period, the S&P 500 (SPY) (IVV) (VOO) has returned 26%, creating a nearly 40-percentage-point performance gap.

The stock now trades below 1.4x estimated Q1 book value, compared to 1.8x a year ago. Longtime Berkshire analyst Chris Bloomstran of Semper Augustus Investments estimates intrinsic value at $855,000 per share - 21% above current levels.

Investor Christopher Davis of Hudson Value Partners calls Berkshire “the ultimate HALO company (heavy assets, low obsolescence),” citing the durability of its insurance business and hard-to-replicate industrial operations including Burlington Northern Santa Fe railroad and Berkshire Hathaway Energy.

UBS analyst Brian Meredith maintains a Buy rating with an $871,000 price target, noting the stock is attractively priced relative to intrinsic value and defensive given its diversified earnings power and $373 billion cash position.

Berkshire resumed share repurchases on March 4 after a nearly two-year hiatus, buying more than $200M of stock. With shares down since then, the company may have accelerated the program.

The company reports earnings on May 2, coinciding with its annual meeting.

data.title: Trending stocks this week as markets weigh war risks and corporate results
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages ended the week mixed, even as the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite climbed to fresh highs as the unresolved conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher.

Crude oil futures rose about 13.2% this week to settle near $94 a barrel, while Brent crude gained 17.2% to around $105 a barrel as of post-market Friday.

On the economic front, retail sales increased more than expected in March, while February business inventories rose 0.4% month over month to $2.69 trillion, ahead of the 0.2% consensus estimate, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

Meanwhile, earnings season gained momentum with a busy slate of reports across sectors from companies including Tesla, Intel, ServiceNow, and defense-related names such as GE Aerospace, Boeing, Honeywell, 3M, Union Pacific, and Lockheed Martin.

For the week, the S&P (SP500) gained +0.6%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) rose +1.5% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) declined -0.4%.

Here’s what caught investor attention this week:

Tesla (TSLA) shares were under pressure Thursday after the company’s Q1 earnings report. The electric vehicle giant reported revenue of $22.9B (+15.8% year-over-year). EPS came in at $0.41 vs. $0.35 consensus and $0.12 a year ago. During the earnings conference call, CEO Elon Musk said 2026 will be a very exciting year. He said the company's heavy capital expenditure spending of about $25B this year will pay off in the future.

Meta Platforms (META) announced an expanded deal with Amazon (AMZN) to deploy "tens of millions" of AWS's Graviton processors to power its AI. Financial details of the deal were not made public, but it is expected to last for at least three years.

Intel (INTC) shares rallied after the chipmaker posted first-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street expectations. For the quarter ended March 28, Intel reported adjusted earnings of $0.29 per share on revenue of $13.58B, up 7.2% year over year, while guidance also came in ahead of expectations. Looking ahead, Intel expects second-quarter revenue to be between $13.8 and $14.8B and an adjusted $0.20 per share.

ServiceNow (NOW) dropped over 17% on Thursday after its quarterly report showed slightly lower-than-expected margins. For the full year, ServiceNow sees a subscription-adjusted gross margin of 81.5%, below the 82.1% estimate, citing recent acquisitions, with normalization expected by 2027.

Apple (AAPL) announced a major leadership transition on April 21, with Tim Cook stepping down as CEO after a 15-year tenure that saw the company’s stock appreciate more than 10x. John Ternus, currently head of hardware engineering, will assume the CEO role on Sept. 1, while Cook transitions to the position of executive chairman.

Lockheed Martin (LMT) was down 4% in early trading on Thursday after the largest U.S. defense contractor and maker of fighter jets, missile systems, and military spacecraft reported dour results, missing Wall Street expectations for both revenue and earnings. Revenue was essentially flat at $17.96B in the first quarter, below analysts’ consensus estimate of $18.25B. Adjusted earnings came in at $6.44 a share, below Wall Street expectations, pressured by unfavorable profit adjustments on the F-16 and C-130 programs as well as weaker margins in the Space unit.

Psychedelic-related stocks rose after President Donald Trump signed an executive order aimed at accelerating research and expanding access to these substances to treat post-traumatic stress disorder. Compass Pathways (CMPS) rose more than 42% on Monday, while AtaiBeckley (ATAI) climbed almost 22%.

Charter Communications (CHTR) fell on Friday after the company released its first-quarter results that included a larger-than-expected loss of broadband customers. The parent company of Spectrum mobile and internet services added 368K new mobile lines (versus +432K estimates) during the quarter but lost 120K Spectrum Internet customers (versus -100K estimates) amid increased promotions from its competitors.

UnitedHealth (UNH) rose after the healthcare company reported better-than-expected Q1 results and increased its full-year earnings outlook. Q1 revenue grew 2% Y/Y to $111.7B, exceeding the consensus by $2.1B, thanks mainly to its insurance segment. Adj. EPS reached $7.23, largely unchanged from Q4 2025 but beating Street forecasts by $0.63.

Marvell Technology (MRVL) gained on Monday after it was reported that the specialty semiconductor company may work with Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) on developing future artificial intelligence chips.

POET Technologies (POET) soared 105.7% over the week after the firm confirmed a business tie-up with Marvell Technologies in an interview with Stocktwits. CFO Thomas Mika told Stocktwits that the company had received a purchase order tied to Marvell (MRVL) and was awaiting responses on additional orders from manufacturing partners and customers, including Foxconn (FXCOF) (HNHAF) (HNHPF) and Luxshare Precision.

Amazon (AMZN)-backed nuclear startup X-Energy (XE) raised nearly $1.02B in an upsized US initial public offering that priced above the marketed range. The Maryland-based company priced more than 44.25M shares at $23 each, above its marketed range of $16 to $19 per share. At that price, the company is valued at about $9.1B based on outstanding shares listed in its filings.

data.title: Real estate stocks underperform as earnings season picks up steam
data.description:
This week, real estate stocks underperformed broader markets as the earnings season picked up steam.

The S&P 500 Real Estate Index Sector (SP500-60) fell 1.41% to 278.83 points, while the accompanying State Street Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLRE) declined 1.42% to $43.83. The broader S&P 500 index outperformed with a 0.47% increase to 7,165.08 points.

The Dow Jones REIT Indx Equity REIT Total Return Index (REIT:IND) retreated 1.32%, while the FTSE Nareit All Equity REITs index was down 1.30%.

Here is a look at the week's notable earnings releases:

  • Digital Realty Trust (DLR) - FFO beats, Revenue beats
  • Rexford Industrial Realty (REXR) - FFO beats, Revenue beats
  • Gaming and Leisure Properties (GLPI) - FFO beats, Revenue beats
  • Alpine Income Property Trust (PINE) - FFO beats, Revenue beats
  • CBRE Group (CBRE) - EPS beats, Revenue beats
  • Crown Castle (CCI) - FFO beats, Revenue beats
  • Essential Properties Realty Trust (EPRT) - FFO beats, Revenue beats
  • Getty Realty (GTY) - FFO beats, Revenue in-line
  • Agree Realty (ADC) - FFO beats, Revenue beats

Weekly Winners & Losers

Among largecap stocks, CoStar Group (CSGP) led the weekly losers, with an 8.26% decline to $36.44. The real estate services provider is set to post its first-quarter earnings next week.

Healthpeak Properties (DOC) followed, retreating 5.41% to close at $16.43.

Equinix (EQIX) (+1.85% W/W to $1,108.76) and AGNC Investment (AGNC) (+1.10% W/W to $11.02) topped the gainers.

AGNC posted stronger-than-expected Q1 earnings this week even as its book value dipped due to volatility in the mortgage-backed securities market at the end of the quarter.

For the midcap losers, Fermi (FRMI) was the biggest decliner, retreating 17.56% to close at $5.40. The stock dropped amid a short call from Fuzzy Panda Research and after the company announced that its CEO and CFO were stepping down.

National Health Investors (NHI) (-10.10% W/W to $77.00) was the next on the list. This week, the health care REIT announced the retirement of CFO John Spaid.

Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) (+4.16% W/W to $5.51), The St. Joe Company (JOE) (+3.94% W/W to $71.01), and Cousins Properties (CUZ) (+3.36% W/W to $24.90) were the top gainers.

Sila Realty Trust (SILA) was the biggest gainer among smallcap stocks, adding 19.08% to $30.40. The week saw SILA announce a definitive merger agreement with certain affiliates of Blue Owl Capital (OWL) involving an all-cash transaction valued at ~$2.4B.

Industrial Logistics Properties Trust (ILPT) (+14.88% W/W to $7.41) followed. The stock benefited from a company announcement about a $1.62B five-year, interest-only mortgage financing at a 5.71% fixed rate.

KKR Real Estate Finance Trust (KREF) was the biggest loser in the category. The stock plunged 10.16% to $6.01 on Q1 earnings miss.

Here is a look at the subsector performances for the week:

data.title: Insider trades: Morgan Stanley, Uber, Adobe among notable names
data.description:
This week insider trades were seen across major firms like Uber (UBER) and Morgan Stanley (MS). The following transactions occurred between April 20, 2026, and April 24, 2026.

Morgan Stanley (MS) Chief Legal and Administrative Officer Eric Grossman sold 11,118 shares at a price range of $190 to $191.5 per share for a total value of $2.12M, reducing his direct holdings by 6.16% to 169,403 shares.

Adobe (ADBE) EVP and CFO Daniel Durn disposed of 3.02% of his direct holdings in the firm. He sold 1,336 shares at $248.02 per share for $331,355, trimming his stake to 42,833 shares.

Tilray Brands (TLRY) director David Hopkinson sold 33,556 shares at $6.94 per share for $232,879, fully exiting his direct position.

U.S. Bancorp (USB) Vice Chair Jodi Richard sold 40,000 shares, reducing her direct holdings by 16.18%. She sold each share at $57.00, totaling $2.28M and reducing her stake to 207,251 shares.

Fluor Corp. disposed of 13.5M shares of NuScale Power (SMR) at $11.81 each for proceeds of $159.4 million, completely exiting its stake in the company.

On the purchase side, Uber (UBER) revealed that its holdings in EV maker Lucid have increased to 37.75M shares, representing an 11.52% stake in the company. It holds the stake indirectly through its wholly owned subsidiary, SMB Holding Corporation.

Vladimir Galkin revealed in a filing that he holds 37.2M shares of JetBlue (JBLU), translating to a 10% stake in the firm.

Baozun (BZUN) CEO Wenbin Qiu purchased 16,000 shares of the company for $3.14 each for a total value of $50,240. The CEO’s indirect holdings have risen to 1.43M shares.
